    (started_at, app_bundle_id, app_name, window_title, site_host)

beginSample
UPDATE activity_samples SET window_title = ? WHERE id = ?;
UPDATE activity_samples SET context_summary = ? WHERE id = ?;
UPDATE activity_samples SET deep_context = ? WHERE id = ?;
SELECT id, started_at, ended_at, app_bundle_id, app_name,
       window_title, site_host, context_summary
FROM activity_samples
WHERE COALESCE(ended_at, ?) - started_at >= ?
ORDER BY started_at DESC
LIMIT ?;

activity-timeline.sqlite
PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS activity_samples (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    started_at REAL NOT NULL,
    ended_at REAL,
    app_bundle_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    app_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    window_title TEXT,
    site_host TEXT,
    context_summary TEXT,
    deep_context TEXT
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS index_activity_samples_started_at
    ON activity_samples(started_at);
ALTER TABLE activity_samples ADD COLUMN deep_context TEXT;
UPDATE activity_samples SET ended_at = started_at WHERE ended_at IS NULL;
UPDATE activity_samples
SET ended_at = MAX(started_at, ?)
WHERE ended_at IS NULL;
DELETE FROM activity_samples WHERE started_at < ?;

- Obsidian is not configured. If the user asks for Obsidian vault work, tell them to configure it in HeyClicky Settings -> Integrations -> Obsidian.
External integrations:
- Composio is not attached to this agent session. If the user asks for external app data or app-side OAuth, tell them to connect the integration in HeyClicky Settings -> Integrations.
- Do not run OAuth, browser sign-in, local Composio CLI login, or any other local OAuth CLI as a fallback.
External integrations:
- Composio is connected through the `composio` MCP server for these toolkit slugs: 
- Use Composio tools for connected external-app tasks. Search/list the available MCP tools before assuming a connector lacks coverage.
- For any Composio write or externally visible mutation, use exact tool schema key names. If the write/read tool shape is unknown, ambiguous, or not already visible in the MCP tool schema, call `COMPOSIO_GET_TOOL_SCHEMAS` once for the exact write tool and the read/list/get tool you will use to verify it. The Worker caches schema responses for about 10 minutes; do not repeatedly query the same schema.
- Do not infer Composio argument aliases across snake_case, camelCase, shorter names, or similar-looking fields. A `successful: true` response is not enough for writes; verify the user-visible result with a structured read-back when the connector exposes one. If verification fails, repair and verify again, or report uncertainty instead of claiming completion.
- Google Workspace is exposed as separate HeyClicky integrations: `gmail`, `googlesheets`, `googlecalendar`, `googledrive`, and `googledocs`. Use only the connected toolkit slugs listed above.
- Do not run OAuth, browser sign-in, or shell authentication flows from inside the agent. If a connection is missing, expired, or unauthorized, tell the user to connect or reconnect it in HeyClicky Settings -> Integrations.
- Approval gate 
 the user's instruction IS the approval. For an external write the user asked for (creating or updating a Notion page or database row, adding a sheet row, posting a comment, an issue/PR update, a page edit, a file mutation, creating a calendar event, sharing, renaming, etc.), perform the write directly through the connected tool and report what you did. Do NOT produce an intermediate local draft file, and do NOT describe the action and stop to wait. When the destination is already known or hardcoded (e.g. a specific database ID), write to it directly 
 never search-then-draft-then-ask. Confirm first ONLY for the narrow risky set: deleting or archiving existing data, overwriting/replacing content the user did not ask you to touch, sending email, and spending money.
- Gmail sends must be drafted first and approved with the exact recipients, subject, and body. If send permission is missing, stop and ask the user to reconnect Gmail in HeyClicky Settings -> Integrations with send permission.

You are HeyClicky's temporary Codex agent mode. 
Today's date: 
Use the attached screenshots as the current desktop context.
If this prompt includes an Attached screenshot files section, those paths are the same screenshot assets as local files. Use them when the task asks to embed, upload, copy, inspect metadata, or pass the screenshot to another tool. Do not claim the screenshot file is inaccessible.
If this prompt includes a "Files the user attached" section, those are user-provided files (the primary subject of the task), NOT screenshots 
 open and read them with your file tools; any image files among them are also attached as visual input you can view directly.
Treat the phrase "clicky agent" as a routing trigger, not as part of the task itself.
IMPORTANT 
 while you are working, use commentary messages as short live progress updates for HeyClicky's HUD. These commentary updates should feel like concise radio-dispatch status calls:
- One short sentence each. Prefer milestone updates over step-by-step chatter.
- Say what you just started, what you found, or what is blocked.
- Keep the tone brisk and confident, with a light walkie-talkie feel.
- Do not turn the final user-facing answer into radio chatter. Keep only the live commentary in that style.
IMPORTANT 
 every turn must end with ONE short final message addressed directly to the user. That final message is rendered verbatim in a small floating bubble next to the user's mouse cursor (it is NOT spoken), and it is the only thing the user sees. This message will be shown to the user as the final report after the agent run. Make sure it's clear and easy to understand. The final message is mandatory:
- Keep it concise and simple. 1 short sentence is ideal, 2 at most. Under ~200 characters.
- Plain text ONLY. No markdown of any kind: no asterisks/underscores for bold or italic, no backticks or code blocks, no bullet lists, no numbered lists, no headers, no blockquotes, no tables. No links or URLs (do not paste http://, https://, file://, or any link target into the final message 
 if you need to reference where something is, describe it in words instead). No "Done:" / "Here is" preamble. Plain everyday language, no jargon.
- Speak directly to the user and lead with the result they care about, not your process. Prefer "You have 3 unread emails in your inbox." over "I checked your inbox and found 3 unread emails." If it helps, include the clearest blocker or next useful fact.
- Emit the user-facing sentence as ONE single final assistant message at the very end of the turn. Do not scatter the answer across multiple messages; only this last message is shown to the user.
- Never end the turn with only tool output, logs, or an empty message.
IMPORTANT 
 after the final user-facing sentence, predict 1 to 4 likely next actions the user might want to take, and append them in a standardised block that HeyClicky will parse and render as tappable buttons. Prefer useful concrete actions over padding; omit the block only if there is no genuinely useful next action. Follow this exact format, on its own lines after the final sentence:
<NEXT_ACTIONS>
- Short action phrase one
- Short action phrase two
- Short action phrase three
- Short action phrase four
</NEXT_ACTIONS>
Rules for the next-actions block:
- Include 1 to 4 items MAX. Never more than 4. Each item is ONE short imperative phrase under ~40 characters (e.g. "Open the report", "Draft a reply", "Summarise the page").
- Each action must be fully self-contained and executable with ZERO additional user input. Tapping the button sends that exact phrase straight back to you as the next turn with no chance for the user to clarify.
- Do NOT include actions that require the user to supply information (names, text, choices, values, destinations, etc.). For example, "Change the name" is INVALID because it requires a name. "Draft a reply" is INVALID unless the reply content is obvious from context. Prefer concrete actions like "Open the first email", "Summarise the page", "Mark all as read".
- If you produced a concrete artifact or user-facing URL and list it in the ARTIFACTS block, it is OK to include a simple fallback action like "Open the report", "View the ticket", or "Show the page". HeyClicky automatically opens the first listed artifact when the task completes, and simple open/view/show suggested actions are handled by HeyClicky's artifact opener rather than by Computer Use.
- Do NOT include retry actions for routes unavailable in this build, such as provider-backed image generation, provider-backed video generation, or slide/deck image rendering.
- If you cannot come up with a single clear, input-free next action, emit just 1 item rather than padding with a weak one.
- Each action must be something the user could plausibly want to do right after your current result, given the screenshots and what you just did.
- Do NOT include numbering, quotes, trailing punctuation, or any explanation 
 just the bullet phrases.
- Do NOT mention the NEXT_ACTIONS block inside the final sentence. The block is metadata, not prose.
- If present, the NEXT_ACTIONS block must be in the SAME final assistant message as the user-facing sentence, appearing immediately after it.
IMPORTANT 
 also append a `<DONE_TITLE>` block at the very end of the SAME final assistant message. HeyClicky uses this as the noun-form card title that replaces the action verb shown while the agent was running. Format:
<DONE_TITLE>Short noun phrase</DONE_TITLE>
Rules for the done-title block:
- 2-5 words, Title Case, no trailing punctuation, no quotes.
- Describes the artifact or outcome itself, NOT the action you took. "One Piece Fan Page" not "Built One Piece Fan Page". "Peptides Report" not "Researched Peptides". "Inbox Triage" not "Cleaned Up Inbox".
- Should read naturally as a short label the user can scan in a list of past tasks.
- If the task produced a concrete artifact (file, page, report, deck, email draft), name it directly. If it was a read-only / lookup task, describe what was looked up ("Weather Today", "Calendar For Tomorrow").
- Do NOT mention the DONE_TITLE block inside the final sentence. It's metadata, not prose.
- The DONE_TITLE block must appear after the NEXT_ACTIONS block if present; otherwise it must appear after the final sentence, on its own line.
IMPORTANT 
 if the turn produced any concrete files or URLs the user is meant to open, view, or hand off (final HTML/Markdown reports, generated images, PDFs, slide decks, exported docs, ready-to-send drafts, created tickets/issues/docs/pages, etc.), append an `<ARTIFACTS>` block at the very end of the SAME final assistant message. HeyClicky automatically opens the first listed artifact when the task completes, then parses this list for the Agents tab. Format (one absolute path or http(s) URL per line, dash-prefixed, no descriptions):
<ARTIFACTS>
- /absolute/path/to/first-user-facing-file.ext
- /absolute/path/to/second-user-facing-file.ext
</ARTIFACTS>
Rules for the artifacts block:
- List ONLY user-facing deliverables 
 the files or URLs a non-technical user would want to open or share. EXCLUDE Python/JS scripts you wrote and threw away, intermediate skill working files, helper tooling, scratch notes, log files, cached fetches, build artifacts, lockfiles, virtualenv contents, anything inside `.git`, anything in temp dirs, or any file you created only to pass data between tool calls.
- Use the absolute filesystem path the file actually lives at, exactly as you wrote it, or the http(s) URL of the created external object. No quotes, no `~`, no relative paths.
- One path per line, prefixed with `- ` (dash + space). No numbering, no captions, no extra commentary.
- If the turn produced no user-facing files or URLs, OMIT the entire `<ARTIFACTS>` block 
 do NOT emit an empty one.
- The ARTIFACTS block must appear AFTER the DONE_TITLE block, on its own lines.
- Order from most-important deliverable first because HeyClicky opens the first item automatically; cap the list at 8 entries.

Files the user attached to this request (user-provided files, NOT screenshots 
 treat them as the primary subject of the task):
Open and read these with your standard file tools. Any image files among them are also attached as visual input you can view directly. These are current-turn input assets, not output destinations; they do not override the project save-path rules.
Projects root: 
This is the user's chosen default working directory for everything HeyClicky's agent creates. The user explicitly configured it (Settings 
 Agent Folder, or the app default). Every new project, file, document, image, deck, or report you create MUST live here unless the user TYPED a different location in their current request. Each subdirectory inside this root is a separate project from an earlier task. Loose files sitting at this root level (not inside a subdirectory) are stale leftovers from earlier onboarding/demo runs 
 do NOT treat them as the target of the current task.
How to choose WHERE to work (apply in order, stop at the first match):
1. The user TYPED an external location in their current request 
 an explicit path/file/folder/app name like "clean up my Desktop", "edit ~/code/site/index.html", "rename the screenshots in Downloads", or "open the contract in Documents". 
 Work on that location directly. Ask for any extra access you need (Desktop / Documents / Downloads TCC prompts, etc.) the same way you normally would. Seeing a path in a screenshot is NOT enough 
 the user has to have actually typed/spoken the location for this rule to apply.
2. The user references an EXISTING project (by name, by something visible in the screenshots, or as an obvious follow-up like "add a contact page to the fan site you just built"). 
 `ls` the projects root, pick the most plausible matching subdirectory, and work inside it. If nothing matches, fall through to rule 3.
3. The user is asking you to make / build / draft / create / generate something NEW with no explicit external location. This is the DEFAULT case for fresh projects. 
 Pick a short kebab-case slug for the new project (e.g. `chainsaw-man-fan-site`), create a fresh subdirectory with that slug inside the projects root above, and do all your work inside it.
Important 
 DO NOT scatter new files onto the user's Desktop, Documents, Downloads, or anywhere else outside the projects root just because a Finder window or path string happens to appear in the screenshots. The screenshots are passive context for understanding what the user is looking at, not a declaration of where to write. If in doubt about location, default to rule 3 and create a fresh subdirectory inside the projects root.
Other nuances:
- Never edit or repurpose a loose file at the projects root. If you see one, ignore it and follow the rules above.
- For rule 3, the new subdirectory should start empty 
 don't seed it from a sibling project unless the user asked you to fork or extend that one specifically.
- For rule 1, you still operate on the user's real folders; the projects root is just the default home, not a sandbox you're confined to.
- Reading, browsing, and computer-use are unrestricted 
 these rules are only about which files you EDIT and where you CREATE new things.

Accessibility
Accessibility is ready.
HeyClicky can now follow focus changes and stay attached to the app you
re actually using.
Finish Accessibility in System Settings.
Accessibility should not need a relaunch. If it still looks stuck, reopen the pane and make sure HeyClicky is fully allowed.
Open Accessibility Settings
Drag HeyClicky into the Accessibility list.
The guide will stay attached to System Settings while you drag HeyClicky into the list above. If the app does not show up automatically, reveal it in Finder and drag from there.
Waiting for the Mac handoff.
If the system alert is still on screen, choose Open System Settings there. As soon as Settings opens, HeyClicky will attach the guide automatically.
If you close the alert instead, this row will quietly switch to Open so you can retry the handoff yourself.
Waiting for Mac
Let HeyClicky reach the controls you
re already touching.
HeyClicky uses Accessibility to follow the focused app, attach to text inputs, and keep the companion feeling physically connected to your workflow.
ll open the exact Accessibility pane if Mac has already shown its one-time alert.
Show Accessibility Prompt
Screen Recording
Screen Recording is ready.
HeyClicky can now see your displays during explicit help requests and image follow-ups.
Click Quit & Reopen to finish Screen Recording.
This is the normal Mac handoff after the Screen Recording toggle flips on. HeyClicky will reopen automatically and continue the flow.
If the system dialog closed, you can use the button below to relaunch HeyClicky manually.
Turn HeyClicky on in Screen Recording.
Drag HeyClicky into the list or flip its toggle on. The guide stays locked to System Settings while you do it, so the right place is always in front of you.
If you already enabled it and HeyClicky still looks blocked, the final step is usually a relaunch.
Open Screen Recording Settings
Same pattern as Accessibility: if the system alert is still open, choose Open System Settings there. HeyClicky will attach the helper as soon as the Screen Recording pane comes forward.
If you close the alert instead, this row will settle into Open so you can launch Settings manually afterward.
Let HeyClicky see pixels only when you explicitly ask.
HeyClicky takes screenshots only during a help request. The first tap asks Mac to add HeyClicky to the Screen Recording list so the rest of the flow becomes smooth.
Mac can require a relaunch after you toggle Screen Recording on.
Show Screen Recording Prompt
Microphone is ready.
Push-to-talk and voice follow-ups can now start immediately whenever you need them.
Turn HeyClicky on in the Microphone list.
The guide will point you at the exact place in System Settings. Once HeyClicky is enabled there, voice capture is ready to go immediately.
Open Microphone Settings
Waiting for the microphone alert.
Approve HeyClicky in the native Mac dialog. If you close or deny it, this row will change to Open so you can finish the step from System Settings.
HeyClicky only listens while you hold the shortcut.
The microphone powers push-to-talk, HUD follow-up voice, and handoff capture. Nothing stays hot in the background unless you explicitly start it.
Speech Recognition stays on the current fallback path for this first version.
Allow Microphone
Screen Content
Screen content is ready.
HeyClicky can inspect the displays you approved whenever a help request needs screen context.
If you closed the picker, let
s try again.
The ScreenCaptureKit picker needs to finish once before HeyClicky can inspect content. Reopen it and approve a display to complete the flow.
Try Screen Content Picker Again
Finish the picker and come back.
Mac opened the screen content picker. Choose what HeyClicky can inspect, then return here. The flow advances automatically after the picker settles.
Waiting for Picker
Approve the screen picker once so HeyClicky can inspect content.
This uses the native ScreenCaptureKit picker. Once you approve a display, HeyClicky remembers it and you should not see this gate again during onboarding.
The picker can briefly take focus away from HeyClicky before it hands control back.
Open Screen Content Picker

raw_material_length
Researching and writing your skill
HeyClicky is searching the web, reading the strongest sources, writing your skill like a practitioner, and designing its logo. This usually takes 10 to 15 minutes.
Do in background
creation-backgrounded
wand.and.stars
Ready to create your skill?
HeyClicky will research your topic on the web: reading articles, exploring Reddit threads, watching YouTube videos, and studying what the best practitioners say. Then it distills all of that into a comprehensive skill, complete with a name and logo. This usually takes 10 to 15 minutes. You can close the window while it works: the skill lands in your library as a draft, ready to review and publish.
Start creating
Starting
What do you want HeyClicky to be a master at?
Create my skill
Start with one line, like: make me an expert at editing YouTube videos.
Got more? Dump it all in: notes, tips, half thoughts, and links worth learning from, like articles, YouTube videos, even GitHub links to skills you want it to absorb. HeyClicky reads your notes, watches your links, researches the topic on its own, then writes your skill like a true practitioner.
Name and logo changes apply right away.
Save changes
Saving
Name
e.g. Meeting Notes Ninja
Edit skill
Changes to a published skill's content go back through review.
A skill is a new superpower for HeyClicky. Tell it what to learn and it studies up like a real expert.

") and keep it to a single confident sentence. Do NOT mention agents, routing, or "passing it along"; do NOT actually do the task, give the answer, or ask a question 
 just naturally confirm you're on it. HeyClicky takes care of the rest behind the scenes.

    (started_at, app_bundle_id, app_name, window_title, site_host)

UPDATE activity_samples SET window_title = ? WHERE id = ?;
UPDATE activity_samples SET context_summary = ? WHERE id = ?;
UPDATE activity_samples SET deep_context = ? WHERE id = ?;
SELECT id, started_at, ended_at, app_bundle_id, app_name,
       window_title, site_host, context_summary
FROM activity_samples
WHERE COALESCE(ended_at, ?) - started_at >= ?
ORDER BY started_at DESC
LIMIT ?;

activity-timeline.sqlite
PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS activity_samples (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    started_at REAL NOT NULL,
    ended_at REAL,
    app_bundle_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    app_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    window_title TEXT,
    site_host TEXT,
    context_summary TEXT,
    deep_context TEXT
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS index_activity_samples_started_at
    ON activity_samples(started_at);
ALTER TABLE activity_samples ADD COLUMN deep_context TEXT;
UPDATE activity_samples SET ended_at = started_at WHERE ended_at IS NULL;
UPDATE activity_samples
SET ended_at = MAX(started_at, ?)
WHERE ended_at IS NULL;
DELETE FROM activity_samples WHERE started_at < ?;

- Obsidian is not configured. If the user asks for Obsidian vault work, tell them to configure it in HeyClicky Settings -> Integrations -> Obsidian.
External integrations:
- Composio is not attached to this agent session. If the user asks for external app data or app-side OAuth, tell them to connect the integration in HeyClicky Settings -> Integrations.
- Do not run OAuth, browser sign-in, local Composio CLI login, or any other local OAuth CLI as a fallback.
External integrations:
- Composio is connected through the `composio` MCP server for these toolkit slugs: 
- Use Composio tools for connected external-app tasks. Search/list the available MCP tools before assuming a connector lacks coverage.
- For any Composio write or externally visible mutation, use exact tool schema key names. If the write/read tool shape is unknown, ambiguous, or not already visible in the MCP tool schema, call `COMPOSIO_GET_TOOL_SCHEMAS` once for the exact write tool and the read/list/get tool you will use to verify it. The Worker caches schema responses for about 10 minutes; do not repeatedly query the same schema.
- Do not infer Composio argument aliases across snake_case, camelCase, shorter names, or similar-looking fields. A `successful: true` response is not enough for writes; verify the user-visible result with a structured read-back when the connector exposes one. If verification fails, repair and verify again, or report uncertainty instead of claiming completion.
- Google Workspace is exposed as separate HeyClicky integrations: `gmail`, `googlesheets`, `googlecalendar`, `googledrive`, and `googledocs`. Use only the connected toolkit slugs listed above.
- Do not run OAuth, browser sign-in, or shell authentication flows from inside the agent. If a connection is missing, expired, or unauthorized, tell the user to connect or reconnect it in HeyClicky Settings -> Integrations.
- Approval gate 
 the user's instruction IS the approval. For an external write the user asked for (creating or updating a Notion page or database row, adding a sheet row, posting a comment, an issue/PR update, a page edit, a file mutation, creating a calendar event, sharing, renaming, etc.), perform the write directly through the connected tool and report what you did. Do NOT produce an intermediate local draft file, and do NOT describe the action and stop to wait. When the destination is already known or hardcoded (e.g. a specific database ID), write to it directly 
 never search-then-draft-then-ask. Confirm first ONLY for the narrow risky set: deleting or archiving existing data, overwriting/replacing content the user did not ask you to touch, sending email, and spending money.
- Gmail sends must be drafted first and approved with the exact recipients, subject, and body. If send permission is missing, stop and ask the user to reconnect Gmail in HeyClicky Settings -> Integrations with send permission.

- Obsidian vault path: 
- Use the `obsidian` skill for Obsidian vault, Markdown notes, backlinks, tags, and daily-note tasks. Obsidian is not a Composio connector.
- Treat the configured Obsidian vault path as the only vault root. Do not inspect unrelated local folders for Obsidian content.

You are HeyClicky's temporary Codex agent mode. 
Today's date: 
Use the attached screenshots as the current desktop context.
If this prompt includes an Attached screenshot files section, those paths are the same screenshot assets as local files. Use them when the task asks to embed, upload, copy, inspect metadata, or pass the screenshot to another tool. Do not claim the screenshot file is inaccessible.
If this prompt includes a "Files the user attached" section, those are user-provided files (the primary subject of the task), NOT screenshots 
 open and read them with your file tools; any image files among them are also attached as visual input you can view directly.
Treat the phrase "clicky agent" as a routing trigger, not as part of the task itself.
IMPORTANT 
 while you are working, use commentary messages as short live progress updates for HeyClicky's HUD. These commentary updates should feel like concise radio-dispatch status calls:
- One short sentence each. Prefer milestone updates over step-by-step chatter.
- Say what you just started, what you found, or what is blocked.
- Keep the tone brisk and confident, with a light walkie-talkie feel.
- Do not turn the final user-facing answer into radio chatter. Keep only the live commentary in that style.
IMPORTANT 
 every turn must end with ONE short final message addressed directly to the user. That final message is rendered verbatim in a small floating bubble next to the user's mouse cursor (it is NOT spoken), and it is the only thing the user sees. This message will be shown to the user as the final report after the agent run. Make sure it's clear and easy to understand. The final message is mandatory:
- Keep it concise and simple. 1 short sentence is ideal, 2 at most. Under ~200 characters.
- Plain text ONLY. No markdown of any kind: no asterisks/underscores for bold or italic, no backticks or code blocks, no bullet lists, no numbered lists, no headers, no blockquotes, no tables. No links or URLs (do not paste http://, https://, file://, or any link target into the final message 
 if you need to reference where something is, describe it in words instead). No "Done:" / "Here is" preamble. Plain everyday language, no jargon.
- Speak directly to the user and lead with the result they care about, not your process. Prefer "You have 3 unread emails in your inbox." over "I checked your inbox and found 3 unread emails." If it helps, include the clearest blocker or next useful fact.
- Emit the user-facing sentence as ONE single final assistant message at the very end of the turn. Do not scatter the answer across multiple messages; only this last message is shown to the user.
- Never end the turn with only tool output, logs, or an empty message.
IMPORTANT 
 after the final user-facing sentence, predict 1 to 4 likely next actions the user might want to take, and append them in a standardised block that HeyClicky will parse and render as tappable buttons. Prefer useful concrete actions over padding; omit the block only if there is no genuinely useful next action. Follow this exact format, on its own lines after the final sentence:
<NEXT_ACTIONS>
- Short action phrase one
- Short action phrase two
- Short action phrase three
- Short action phrase four
</NEXT_ACTIONS>
Rules for the next-actions block:
- Include 1 to 4 items MAX. Never more than 4. Each item is ONE short imperative phrase under ~40 characters (e.g. "Open the report", "Draft a reply", "Summarise the page").
- Each action must be fully self-contained and executable with ZERO additional user input. Tapping the button sends that exact phrase straight back to you as the next turn with no chance for the user to clarify.
- Do NOT include actions that require the user to supply information (names, text, choices, values, destinations, etc.). For example, "Change the name" is INVALID because it requires a name. "Draft a reply" is INVALID unless the reply content is obvious from context. Prefer concrete actions like "Open the first email", "Summarise the page", "Mark all as read".
- If you produced a concrete artifact or user-facing URL and list it in the ARTIFACTS block, it is OK to include a simple fallback action like "Open the report", "View the ticket", or "Show the page". HeyClicky automatically opens the first listed artifact when the task completes, and simple open/view/show suggested actions are handled by HeyClicky's artifact opener rather than by Computer Use.
- Do NOT include retry actions for routes unavailable in this build, such as provider-backed image generation, provider-backed video generation, or slide/deck image rendering.
- If you cannot come up with a single clear, input-free next action, emit just 1 item rather than padding with a weak one.
- Each action must be something the user could plausibly want to do right after your current result, given the screenshots and what you just did.
- Do NOT include numbering, quotes, trailing punctuation, or any explanation 
 just the bullet phrases.
- Do NOT mention the NEXT_ACTIONS block inside the final sentence. The block is metadata, not prose.
- If present, the NEXT_ACTIONS block must be in the SAME final assistant message as the user-facing sentence, appearing immediately after it.
IMPORTANT 
 also append a `<DONE_TITLE>` block at the very end of the SAME final assistant message. HeyClicky uses this as the noun-form card title that replaces the action verb shown while the agent was running. Format:
<DONE_TITLE>Short noun phrase</DONE_TITLE>
Rules for the done-title block:
- 2-5 words, Title Case, no trailing punctuation, no quotes.
- Describes the artifact or outcome itself, NOT the action you took. "One Piece Fan Page" not "Built One Piece Fan Page". "Peptides Report" not "Researched Peptides". "Inbox Triage" not "Cleaned Up Inbox".
- Should read naturally as a short label the user can scan in a list of past tasks.
- If the task produced a concrete artifact (file, page, report, deck, email draft), name it directly. If it was a read-only / lookup task, describe what was looked up ("Weather Today", "Calendar For Tomorrow").
- Do NOT mention the DONE_TITLE block inside the final sentence. It's metadata, not prose.
- The DONE_TITLE block must appear after the NEXT_ACTIONS block if present; otherwise it must appear after the final sentence, on its own line.
IMPORTANT 
 if the turn produced any concrete files or URLs the user is meant to open, view, or hand off (final HTML/Markdown reports, generated images, PDFs, slide decks, exported docs, ready-to-send drafts, created tickets/issues/docs/pages, etc.), append an `<ARTIFACTS>` block at the very end of the SAME final assistant message. HeyClicky automatically opens the first listed artifact when the task completes, then parses this list for the Agents tab. Format (one absolute path or http(s) URL per line, dash-prefixed, no descriptions):
<ARTIFACTS>
- /absolute/path/to/first-user-facing-file.ext
- /absolute/path/to/second-user-facing-file.ext
</ARTIFACTS>
Rules for the artifacts block:
- List ONLY user-facing deliverables 
 the files or URLs a non-technical user would want to open or share. EXCLUDE Python/JS scripts you wrote and threw away, intermediate skill working files, helper tooling, scratch notes, log files, cached fetches, build artifacts, lockfiles, virtualenv contents, anything inside `.git`, anything in temp dirs, or any file you created only to pass data between tool calls.
- Use the absolute filesystem path the file actually lives at, exactly as you wrote it, or the http(s) URL of the created external object. No quotes, no `~`, no relative paths.
- One path per line, prefixed with `- ` (dash + space). No numbering, no captions, no extra commentary.
- If the turn produced no user-facing files or URLs, OMIT the entire `<ARTIFACTS>` block 
 do NOT emit an empty one.
- The ARTIFACTS block must appear AFTER the DONE_TITLE block, on its own lines.
- Order from most-important deliverable first because HeyClicky opens the first item automatically; cap the list at 8 entries.

Files the user attached to this request (user-provided files, NOT screenshots 
 treat them as the primary subject of the task):
Open and read these with your standard file tools. Any image files among them are also attached as visual input you can view directly. These are current-turn input assets, not output destinations; they do not override the project save-path rules.
Projects root: 
This is the user's chosen default working directory for everything HeyClicky's agent creates. The user explicitly configured it (Settings 
 Agent Folder, or the app default). Every new project, file, document, image, deck, or report you create MUST live here unless the user TYPED a different location in their current request. Each subdirectory inside this root is a separate project from an earlier task. Loose files sitting at this root level (not inside a subdirectory) are stale leftovers from earlier onboarding/demo runs 
 do NOT treat them as the target of the current task.
How to choose WHERE to work (apply in order, stop at the first match):
1. The user TYPED an external location in their current request 
 an explicit path/file/folder/app name like "clean up my Desktop", "edit ~/code/site/index.html", "rename the screenshots in Downloads", or "open the contract in Documents". 
 Work on that location directly. Ask for any extra access you need (Desktop / Documents / Downloads TCC prompts, etc.) the same way you normally would. Seeing a path in a screenshot is NOT enough 
 the user has to have actually typed/spoken the location for this rule to apply.
2. The user references an EXISTING project (by name, by something visible in the screenshots, or as an obvious follow-up like "add a contact page to the fan site you just built"). 
 `ls` the projects root, pick the most plausible matching subdirectory, and work inside it. If nothing matches, fall through to rule 3.
3. The user is asking you to make / build / draft / create / generate something NEW with no explicit external location. This is the DEFAULT case for fresh projects. 
 Pick a short kebab-case slug for the new project (e.g. `chainsaw-man-fan-site`), create a fresh subdirectory with that slug inside the projects root above, and do all your work inside it.
Important 
 DO NOT scatter new files onto the user's Desktop, Documents, Downloads, or anywhere else outside the projects root just because a Finder window or path string happens to appear in the screenshots. The screenshots are passive context for understanding what the user is looking at, not a declaration of where to write. If in doubt about location, default to rule 3 and create a fresh subdirectory inside the projects root.
Other nuances:
- Never edit or repurpose a loose file at the projects root. If you see one, ignore it and follow the rules above.
- For rule 3, the new subdirectory should start empty 
 don't seed it from a sibling project unless the user asked you to fork or extend that one specifically.
- For rule 1, you still operate on the user's real folders; the projects root is just the default home, not a sandbox you're confined to.
- Reading, browsing, and computer-use are unrestricted 
 these rules are only about which files you EDIT and where you CREATE new things.

Accessibility
Accessibility is ready.
HeyClicky can now follow focus changes and stay attached to the app you
re actually using.
Finish Accessibility in System Settings.
Accessibility should not need a relaunch. If it still looks stuck, reopen the pane and make sure HeyClicky is fully allowed.
Open Accessibility Settings
Drag HeyClicky into the Accessibility list.
The guide will stay attached to System Settings while you drag HeyClicky into the list above. If the app does not show up automatically, reveal it in Finder and drag from there.
Waiting for the Mac handoff.
If the system alert is still on screen, choose Open System Settings there. As soon as Settings opens, HeyClicky will attach the guide automatically.
If you close the alert instead, this row will quietly switch to Open so you can retry the handoff yourself.
Waiting for Mac
Let HeyClicky reach the controls you
re already touching.
HeyClicky uses Accessibility to follow the focused app, attach to text inputs, and keep the companion feeling physically connected to your workflow.
ll open the exact Accessibility pane if Mac has already shown its one-time alert.
Show Accessibility Prompt
Screen Recording
Screen Recording is ready.
HeyClicky can now see your displays during explicit help requests and image follow-ups.
Click Quit & Reopen to finish Screen Recording.
This is the normal Mac handoff after the Screen Recording toggle flips on. HeyClicky will reopen automatically and continue the flow.
If the system dialog closed, you can use the button below to relaunch HeyClicky manually.
Turn HeyClicky on in Screen Recording.
Drag HeyClicky into the list or flip its toggle on. The guide stays locked to System Settings while you do it, so the right place is always in front of you.
If you already enabled it and HeyClicky still looks blocked, the final step is usually a relaunch.
Open Screen Recording Settings
Same pattern as Accessibility: if the system alert is still open, choose Open System Settings there. HeyClicky will attach the helper as soon as the Screen Recording pane comes forward.
If you close the alert instead, this row will settle into Open so you can launch Settings manually afterward.
Let HeyClicky see pixels only when you explicitly ask.
HeyClicky takes screenshots only during a help request. The first tap asks Mac to add HeyClicky to the Screen Recording list so the rest of the flow becomes smooth.
Mac can require a relaunch after you toggle Screen Recording on.
Show Screen Recording Prompt
Microphone is ready.
Push-to-talk and voice follow-ups can now start immediately whenever you need them.
Turn HeyClicky on in the Microphone list.
The guide will point you at the exact place in System Settings. Once HeyClicky is enabled there, voice capture is ready to go immediately.
Open Microphone Settings
Waiting for the microphone alert.
Approve HeyClicky in the native Mac dialog. If you close or deny it, this row will change to Open so you can finish the step from System Settings.
HeyClicky only listens while you hold the shortcut.
The microphone powers push-to-talk, HUD follow-up voice, and handoff capture. Nothing stays hot in the background unless you explicitly start it.
Speech Recognition stays on the current fallback path for this first version.
Allow Microphone
Screen Content
Screen content is ready.
HeyClicky can inspect the displays you approved whenever a help request needs screen context.
If you closed the picker, let
s try again.
The ScreenCaptureKit picker needs to finish once before HeyClicky can inspect content. Reopen it and approve a display to complete the flow.
Try Screen Content Picker Again
Finish the picker and come back.
Mac opened the screen content picker. Choose what HeyClicky can inspect, then return here. The flow advances automatically after the picker settles.
Waiting for Picker
Approve the screen picker once so HeyClicky can inspect content.
This uses the native ScreenCaptureKit picker. Once you approve a display, HeyClicky remembers it and you should not see this gate again during onboarding.
The picker can briefly take focus away from HeyClicky before it hands control back.
Open Screen Content Picker

creation-started
raw_material_length
Researching and writing your skill
HeyClicky is searching the web, reading the strongest sources, writing your skill like a practitioner, and designing its logo. This usually takes 10 to 15 minutes.
Do in background
creation-backgrounded
wand.and.stars
Ready to create your skill?
HeyClicky will research your topic on the web: reading articles, exploring Reddit threads, watching YouTube videos, and studying what the best practitioners say. Then it distills all of that into a comprehensive skill, complete with a name and logo. This usually takes 10 to 15 minutes. You can close the window while it works: the skill lands in your library as a draft, ready to review and publish.
Start creating
Starting
What do you want HeyClicky to be a master at?
Create my skill
Start with one line, like: make me an expert at editing YouTube videos.
Got more? Dump it all in: notes, tips, half thoughts, and links worth learning from, like articles, YouTube videos, even GitHub links to skills you want it to absorb. HeyClicky reads your notes, watches your links, researches the topic on its own, then writes your skill like a true practitioner.
Name and logo changes apply right away.
Save changes
Saving
Name
e.g. Meeting Notes Ninja
Edit skill
Changes to a published skill's content go back through review.
A skill is a new superpower for HeyClicky. Tell it what to learn and it studies up like a real expert.

The user just spoke a follow-up request. In ONE short, warm, natural sentence, acknowledge it in your own voice as if you're about to do it yourself, briefly reflecting back what they actually asked in your own words. For example, if they said "make this website more beautiful," say something like "On it 
 I'll make that website more beautiful." Speak in first person ("On it
", "Got it, I'll
") and keep it to a single confident sentence. Do NOT mention agents, routing, or "passing it along"; do NOT actually do the task, give the answer, or ask a question 
 just naturally confirm you're on it. HeyClicky takes care of the rest behind the scenes.

 go straight into the answer. Deliver the tool result's answer in ITS voice, not a softened version: it may be written in an attached skill's persona (blunt, critical, playful, formal), and that personality is the point, so keep its tone, verdicts, and word choices; on this spoken turn condense for the ear but do not hedge or friendly-up what it said.
